title: Hack The Box — Lame
description: Classic entry-level box exploiting a vulnerable Samba version via an unauthenticated command injection flaw.

title: Hack The Box — Legacy
description: Windows XP machine vulnerable to MS08-067 and EternalBlue. A staple for learning Windows exploitation fundamentals.

title: Hack The Box — Blue
description: Windows 7 box vulnerable to EternalBlue (MS17-010). Great for understanding the NSA exploit without Metasploit.

title: Hack The Box — Jerry
description: Apache Tomcat with default credentials allowing a malicious WAR file upload for remote code execution.

title: Hack The Box — Netmon
description: PRTG Network Monitor exposed via anonymous FTP. Exploiting a command injection in the alert notification feature.

title: Hack The Box — Bank
description: Linux web box requiring DNS enumeration to discover a virtual host, then bypassing upload filters to get a shell.

title: Hack The Box — Active
description: Active Directory box abusing Group Policy Preferences password decryption and Kerberoasting to escalate to Domain Admin.

title: Hack The Box — Devel
description: Writable FTP root mapped to an IIS web directory. Initial foothold via ASPX webshell, privilege escalation with a kernel exploit.

title: Hack The Box — Bastard
description: Drupal 7 CMS vulnerable to Drupalgeddon SQL injection. Privilege escalation via MS15-051 token impersonation.

title: Hack The Box — Beep
description: Elastix VOIP server with multiple attack vectors including LFI and Shellshock. Multiple paths to root make it great for practice.

title: Hack The Box — Shocker
description: Web server running CGI scripts vulnerable to Shellshock (CVE-2014-6271). Privilege escalation via sudo perl.

title: Hack The Box — Nibbles
description: NibbleBlog CMS with a file upload vulnerability after guessing weak credentials. Sudo misconfiguration leads to root.

title: Hack The Box — Grandpa
description: IIS 6.0 running WebDAV vulnerable to a buffer overflow. Privilege escalation via token kidnapping / Churrasco.

title: Hack The Box — Valentine
description: Linux box themed around the Heartbleed vulnerability. Memory leak leaks an encoded SSH key. Root via tmux session hijacking.

title: Hack The Box — Poison
description: FreeBSD web server with LFI leading to log poisoning for RCE. Root via SSH port forwarding to an exposed VNC session.

title: Hack The Box — Cronos
description: DNS zone transfer reveals subdomains. SQLi bypasses login, command injection gives shell. Root via writable cron job.

title: Hack The Box — SolidState
description: Apache James mail server with default credentials allows setting a malicious mail forward for RCE. Writable cron script leads to root.

title: Hack The Box — Sunday
description: Solaris box using the old finger protocol for user enumeration. SSH brute force and sudo wget abuse for privilege escalation.

title: Hack The Box — Node
description: Node.js app leaking credentials via an unprotected API endpoint. Privilege escalation via a custom SUID binary buffer overflow.

title: Hack The Box — Forest
description: Active Directory machine exploitable via AS-REP Roasting against accounts without pre-auth. DCSync via Exchange Windows Permissions ACL abuse.

title: Hack The Box — Sauna
description: User enumeration from a corporate website leads to AS-REP Roasting. AutoLogon credentials in registry allow lateral movement to DCSync.

title: Hack The Box — Bucket
description: Local AWS environment with an exposed S3 bucket allowing webshell upload. DynamoDB credential leak leads to lateral movement and root.

title: TryHackMe — Kenobi
description: Enumerating Samba shares and exploiting a ProFTPd vulnerability to copy an SSH key. SUID binary path hijacking for root.

title: TryHackMe — Steel Mountain
description: HttpFileServer 2.3 vulnerable to remote code execution. Privilege escalation via an unquoted service path.

title: TryHackMe — Alfred
description: Jenkins with default credentials allows running a build job to execute a PowerShell reverse shell. Token impersonation for SYSTEM.

title: TryHackMe — HackPark
description: Brute forcing BlogEngine.NET login with Hydra, then exploiting a directory traversal RCE. Privilege escalation via unquoted service path.

title: TryHackMe — Game Zone
description: SQL injection for authentication bypass, sqlmap to dump hashes, and SSH tunneling to reach an internal Webmin instance for root.

title: TryHackMe — Skynet
description: Samba share leaks credentials for a SquirrelMail instance. Remote file inclusion in Cuppa CMS. Root via a wildcard cron tar exploit.

title: TryHackMe — Anonymous
description: Anonymous FTP access reveals a writable cron script. SUID enumeration with find provides an easy path to root.
